compiled_state.title: As the fix-bug orchestrator, I enforce RED-before-fix as a hard gate
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Add a hard gate in the fix-bug orchestrator that prevents code-modifying actions (Edit, Write, fix implementation sub-agent dispatch) before a RED test has been written and confirmed failing.
**Why**: Agents sometimes jump to implementation without writing a failing test that demonstrates the root cause. This gate ensures TDD discipline.
**Scope**:
- IN: Gate between Step 5 (RED test) and Step 6 (fix implementation), mechanical bug exemption
- OUT: Unit tests (story 8a52-74f7)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the fix-bug orchestrator blocks fix implementation dispatch when no RED test has been written or run
  ← Satisfies: "No code-modifying action occurs before a RED test demonstrates the root cause"
- When this story is complete, mechanical bugs (import errors, lint violations, config syntax) bypass the RED gate via the existing Mechanical Fix Path
  ← Satisfies: "Mechanical bugs are exempt per the existing Mechanical Fix Path"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Gate must distinguish mechanical vs behavioral classification from Step 1
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT: Step 1 complete. Read ticket and SKILL.md. Step 5 is at line 384, Step 6 at line 401. Mechanical Fix Path is at line 64-70. Plan: (1) write tests verifying gate language exists between Step 5 and Step 6, (2) add gate block to SKILL.md between Step 5 and Step 6 that blocks fix implementation when no RED test written/confirmed failing, with mechanical bug exemption.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT: Step 2 complete. Reviewed SKILL.md and existing tests in test_fix_bug_skill.py. Structure is clear: Step 5 (RED Test) ends at line ~399, Step 6 (Fix Implementation) starts at line 401. Gate needs to go between them. Mechanical Fix Path is exempted because it bypasses Steps 1-5 entirely and routes directly to fixing. Will now write failing tests for the RED-before-fix gate.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT: Step 3 complete. Wrote 5 failing tests in TestRedBeforeFixGate class in tests/skills/test_fix_bug_skill.py. All 5 confirmed RED. Tests verify: (1) gate present, (2) gate blocks fix without RED test, (3) gate requires confirmed failing test, (4) gate exempts mechanical bugs, (5) gate positioned between Step 5 and Step 6. Now implementing the gate in SKILL.md.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT: Implementation complete. Added Step 5.5 RED-before-fix Gate to SKILL.md between Step 5 and Step 6. Gate: blocks Edit/Write/fix dispatch without confirmed failing RED test, exempts mechanical bugs. All 48 tests in test_fix_bug_skill.py pass (5 new + 43 pre-existing). AC check: (1) gate blocks fix implementation when no RED test confirmed - PASS; (2) mechanical bugs exempt via Mechanical Fix Path - PASS; (3) unit tests written and passing - PASS.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: Added Step 5.5 RED-before-fix Gate. Blocks code modification until RED test confirmed failing. Mechanical bugs exempt. 5 tests pass.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
